NUMEROUS STREETS ARE FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE IN LINCOLN DUE TO HIGH WATER. FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS BARRICADING ROADS IN TOWN. A RESIDENT IN TOWN ALSO REPORTED BASEMENT FLOODING OF THEIR HOME.

THERE ARE ONGOING WATER RESCUES IN THE NORTHEAST PART OF THE COUNTY. OHIO STREET NORTH OF INTERSTATE 70 IS IMPASSABLE. A HOUSING DEVELOPMENT HAS WATER IMPACTING MULTIPLE HOMES. ONE HOME HAS FOUR FEET OF WATER IN THE BASEMENT.
MAIN STREET IN TOWN IS IMPASSABLE. EVACUATIONS HAVE OCCURRED IN TOWN. NO WATER RESCUES THUS FAR. THE WATER IS STILL RISING.
WATER RESCUE FROM HOME FOR 2 ADULTS AND 2 CHILDREN JUST WEST OF PARADISE.
NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS ARE WATER COVERED. VOLUNTARY EVACUATIONS OCCURRED IN PARADISE AND ALONG PARADISE CREEK.

DELAYED REPORT. 4 POWER POLES SNAPPED IN HALF 3 MILES E OF LEON ON KS 400.

THE FLOODWATERS HAVE RECEDED BACK INTO WHISKEY CREEK AND THE ONLY ROAD CLOSED IS NOW 19TH AND MAPLE AND 800 BLK OF NORTH PARK BLVD. TAYLOR ROAD IS CLOSED BY THE RAILROAD TRACK GOING TOWARDS PETERPAN IN THE COUNTY WITH WATER OVER THE ROAD. TWO ACCIDENTS WERE RELATED TO HEAVY RAINFALL AND HYDROPLANING. IN INDEPENDENCE WATER MADE IT UP TO HOUSES IN A FEW AREAS BUT THERE WAS NO PROPERTY DAMAGE. 2 VEHICLES TOWED THAT WERE STALLED OUT IN HIGH WATER. UNOFFICIAL - 3.25 OF RAIN IN THE DOWNTOWN AREA OF INDEPENDENCE SINCE THIS A.M.
COUNTY CREWS ARE OUT CLOSING MULTIPLE ROADS, SHE SAID MAINLY IN THE NORTH END OF THE COUNTY.
